calculateSignPrice.name: Calculate Sign Price
calculateSignPrice.description: Write a function that calculates the price of a sign. Each letter costs $12. Spaces are free and should not be counted. Return the result as a formatted string.

simpleWord.name: Simple word
simpleWord.description: A single word with no spaces.

singleLetter.name: Single letter
singleLetter.description: A sign with just one letter.

twoWords.name: Two words
twoWords.description: Two words with a space that should be skipped.

multipleSpaces.name: Multiple spaces
multipleSpaces.description: Multiple words with several spaces to skip.

longText.name: Long text
longText.description: A longer sign with more letters.

allSpaces.name: All spaces
allSpaces.description: A sign with only spaces has no letters to charge for.

whatCounting.question: What am I actually counting?
whatCounting.answer: Letters. Specifically, anything that isn't a space. So you want a counter that goes up by 1 for each non-space character in the sign text.

loopCharacters.question: How do I loop through the characters?
loopCharacters.answer: A for-each loop gives you each character in turn. Inside, an if-statement checks whether it's a space. Only count it if it isn't.

workOutPrice.question: How do I work out the price?
workOutPrice.answer: Once the loop ends, multiply your letter count by 12. That's the cost.

buildResultString.question: How do I build the result string?
buildResultString.answer: The price is a number, but the return value is a string. Use `numberToString()` to convert the price to a string, then use concatenation (`+`) or a template string to add any surrounding text the exercise asks for.
